extra.reasoning: The only transition that can set sent:=true while opted_out is true is t_resume_glitch, guarded by (opted_out and acc==13). I verified (a) the triple-nested if-then-else reduction in each step exactly equals (3*acc + c) mod 64 for all acc in [0,63] and c in {8,6,52} (since 3*63+52=241 < 64*4, three conditional subtractions fully reduce), and (b) the full set of acc values reachable from the initial acc=0 under steps step_0/step_1/step_2 is exactly the 32 even values {0,2,4,...,62}. Target acc==13 is odd and never appears. This is an invariant: acc starts even (0); each step computes 3*acc + c with c even (8/6/52) and reduces mod 64 (even), so 3*even+even=even stays even. Hence the t_resume_glitch guard (acc==13) is never satisfiable on any reachable execution. All other send paths while opted out hit t_send_blocked (sent:=false); t_send_allowed requires not opted_out; optout and every step set sent:=false. Therefore globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. No counterexample exists; the target is unreachable.
